_why: Governs all memory behaviour for AI-OS runtime. Defines load triggers, compression policy, conflict resolution, expiration, and token budgeting. Designed for long-running technical sessions with cross-session project continuity.

load_policy._why: Memory loading is not free — each retrieval costs latency and RPM. Only load when user context materially changes the answer.
auto_load_triggers: user references a project by name
user says 'as we discussed' or equivalent
question involves previously established naming conventions
skip_triggers: generic factual questions with no personal dependency
first message of a new session with no project context signal

compression_policy._why: Raw conversation history grows linearly. Without compression, long sessions hit the 65,536 token ceiling and degrade quality. Compression preserves decisions, not transcripts.

summarization_policy._why: Summarization reduces token budget pressure while keeping decision context. Triggered at the context ceiling, not on a fixed schedule.
priority_preserve: code that was accepted by user

cache_policy._why: Caching avoids redundant retrieval calls for items already loaded this session. Reduces RPM consumption.
cache_invalidation_triggers: user explicitly overrides a cached decision
user uploads a new document contradicting cached content

conflict_resolution._why: Memory entries can become stale or contradicted by new user input. Explicit resolution protocol prevents silent inconsistencies.
priority_order: explicit user instruction in current turn
conflict_resolution.conflict_surface_protocol: This differs from [prior decision: {summary}]. Do you want to update or override it?

expiration_policy._why: Stale decisions cause incorrect recommendations. Version-tagged entries expire when their version is superseded.

token_budget._why: Memory context must not crowd out the actual conversation. Hard ceiling prevents context overflow from memory alone.

forbidden_content: PII (names, emails, phone numbers outside project context)
